Phase-47.NAME: MTProto CTA Subdomain Link Generation
Phase-47.purpose: Implement marketing-oriented CTA subdomains for newly issued personal MTProto proxy links while preserving existing working proxies and the Phase-41 RU/DE fake-TLS data-plane.

scope.in: CTA prefix allow-list, 7-hex public user suffix derivation, explicit prefix validation, stable pseudo-random prefix choice, CTA SNI generation, owner payload consistency, backend tests, frontend/smoke guards, and MyGRACE metadata.
scope.out: Deploy/install scripts, nginx/docker-compose, wildcard TLS issuance, database schema migrations, MTProto runtime topology, promotion tag runtime, and automatic mass reissue of existing u-* assignments.

steps.step-1: Add the fixed CTA prefix allow-list: kupi-vpn, vpn-tut, beri-vpn, bez-blokirovok, hochu-bystree, and krot-vpn.
steps.step-2: Implement public short-id derivation: use first 7 hex characters for hex-like user keys and a stable 7-hex hash suffix for numeric internal IDs.
steps.step-3: Implement explicit prefix validation and stable pseudo-random prefix selection without accepting arbitrary DNS labels.
steps.step-4: Generate wildcard-safe CTA SNI as cta-prefix-shortid.base-domain with collision nonce support and DNS label validation.
steps.step-5: Integrate CTA SNI into new assignment creation while preserving existing active u-* assignments unless reissue is explicit.
steps.step-6: Verify fake-TLS secret derivation, live policy apply expectations, and RU SNI router compatibility for the final CTA SNI.
steps.step-7: Ensure user-cabinet tg:// and https://t.me links both use the CTA server value returned by the owner API.
steps.step-8: Add targeted backend tests and Phase-47 static smoke for prefix allow-list, short-id derivation, link consistency, redaction, and protected deploy surfaces.
steps.step-9: Run backend provisioning tests, frontend build, Phase-47 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and deploy/install diff guard.

data-flow.flow-1: Verified user -> MTProtoProvisioningService -> CTA prefix selector -> public 7-hex suffix -> cta-prefix-shortid.krotpn.xyz.
data-flow.flow-2: CTA SNI -> derive_fake_tls_secret -> owner payload server/secret -> tg://proxy and https://t.me/proxy links.
data-flow.flow-3: CTA SNI assignment -> runtime policy apply -> RU SNI router -> DE KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: A newly verified user receives kupi-vpn-4bb40fa.krotpn.xyz; Telegram sees the same server in the fake-TLS secret and can connect through the existing RU/DE edge.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: An existing user with u-0195616cec89.krotpn.xyz logs in after Phase-47 and keeps the old working assignment because no automatic mass reissue occurs.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: An invalid explicit prefix such as custom-domain is rejected before SNI creation, so arbitrary hostnames cannot enter policy apply or logs.

phase-gates.gate-1: CTA hostnames match one of the approved prefixes plus a 7-hex suffix under the configured base domain.
phase-gates.gate-2: Raw numeric user IDs are not visible in generated public hostnames.
phase-gates.gate-3: Existing active assignments remain stable and are not silently rotated.
phase-gates.gate-4: Derived ee fake-TLS secret contains the final CTA SNI hex and does not use stale u-* server text for new assignments.

risks.risk-1: CTA hostnames are more marketing-visible than neutral u-* hostnames, which may improve recall but can make blocked-word tuning more sensitive.
risks.risk-2: Changing SNI generation breaks old proxy links if existing assignments are silently rewritten; Phase-47 forbids automatic mass rotation.
risks.risk-3: Secret derivation must use the final CTA SNI because Telegram fake-TLS secret embeds the hostname in hex.
risks.risk-4: Random prefix choice must be deterministic after issuance; true randomness belongs only to create/reissue time and must be persisted as SNI.

matrix.module-local: Backend tests validate CTA prefix allow-list, short-id derivation for hex-like and numeric IDs, DNS label safety, explicit prefix rejection, collision nonce behavior, fake-TLS secret derivation from final SNI, and legacy u-* assignment preservation.
matrix.wave-level: Frontend build and Phase-47 smoke validate owner payload consistency: server, secret, tg:// link, and https://t.me link all use the final CTA SNI without leaking real full links.
matrix.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose diff guard validate metadata consistency and operational isolation.
matrix.runtime-safety: Runtime topology is not changed; policy apply expectations are verified by existing M-049/M-050 contracts and static checks that CTA SNI remains one wildcard-safe label under the configured base domain.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-47.xml docs/modules/M-065.xml docs/verification/V-M-065.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/current-status.xml

verification.evidence: 2026-06-01: backend provisioning tests passed 14/14 outside sandbox after sandbox aiosqlite hang, frontend build passed, Phase-47 smoke passed, protected deploy/install diff guard had no output, XML parse passed, and MyGRACE lint passed.
